name: Transgender pride flag

colors.symbolism: the traditional colour for baby boys
the traditional colour for baby girls
those who are transitioning, intersex, or who consider themselves non-binary

description: Five horizontal stripes in a palindromic pattern — from top to bottom: light blue, pink, white, pink, light blue.
history: Designed by US Navy veteran Monica Helms in 1999 and first flown at the Phoenix Pride parade in 2000. The palindromic stripe order is deliberate: 'the pattern is such that no matter which way you fly it, it will always be correct, signifying us trying to find correctness in our own lives.'

features.symbolism: the palindromic stripe order is intentional so the flag is correct flown either way
